name: Assisted Transportation Corp
recent_news: Holds Common authority since 2022 per FMCSA; small NEMT/wheelchair operator. Not to be confused with PE-backed Assisted Transportation Services (MO/KS, owned by ASTP/CVC DIF).
fit_notes: Tiny Westchester-area NEMT/wheelchair operator (~10 EE, ~$800K rev), primarily senior/medical transport, not a true school-bus contractor. Limited Summit fit absent a special-needs/IEP route book — better as a wheelchair-fleet bolt-on if expanding alt-transport in NY.

name: Koch Transportation Svc LLC
recent_news: Listed as minority/Black-owned business; one Yelp listing shows 'CLOSED' though Alignable and FMCSA records suggest active NEMT operation. Primary service is medical/dialysis/rehab transport, not school routes.
fit_notes: Sub-scale Buford-area NEMT van operator (~10 EE, ~$780K rev), no confirmed school district contracts. Not a school-bus tuck-in candidate; weak fit for Summit.

name: Comfort Care Transport
districts_or_customers: Bartow County / Cartersville-area disabled and special-needs riders
recent_news: Small owner-operated NEMT/disabled-persons transport. Note: a separate Comfort Care Transportation Services Inc (founded by Mohammad Naeem) operates a different brand; the Cartersville entity at 32 Lake Top Dr appears distinct.
fit_notes: Very small owner-operated disabled-persons transport (~11 EE, ~$725K rev) in NW Georgia; no confirmed school-bus route contracts. Weak Summit fit unless bundled into a broader Atlanta-region SPED rollup.

name: Atlanta Metro Transport Svc
recent_news: Established 2016 to serve riders who cannot self-transport due to age or disability; ambulatory, wheelchair and stretcher service. FMCSA DOT 3902769; small operator with no public school contracts surfaced.
fit_notes: Single-tier NEMT operator south of Atlanta (~10 EE, ~$600K rev); medical-trip focus, not school routes. Weak Summit tuck-in unless layered with other SPED/alt-transport buys in metro ATL.

name: Dousman Transport Co
districts_or_customers: ~10,000 students/day per one source; up to ~30,000 students across all terminals per another
recent_news: Founded 1962 by Robert Southcott; acquired 1988 by long-time employee Magda Dimmendaal (Dutch-born, started as driver in 1973). Acquired Lenon Bus in 2007. Operates 10+ Wisconsin terminals; remains independent family-owned.
fit_notes: Strong Summit tuck-in: 60+ year SE Wisconsin platform, ~$14M rev / 450 EE / 10 terminals serving 8+ districts (anchor: Kettle Moraine). Owner Magda Dimmendaal acquired in 1988 — likely late-career, high succession-signal. Would extend Summit footprint into WI and bring established district relationships and prior acquisition track record (Lenon Bus 2007).
